1: A shadow: "The LORD reigns; let the earth rejoice." Revelation 11:15 — "the kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and of his Christ, and he shall reign forever and ever." Revelation 19:6 — "Hallelujah! For the Lord our God the Almighty reigns." The enthronement of the Lord announced in the Psalter is the enthronement of Christ at his resurrection and exaltation (Acts 2:36 — "God has made him both Lord and Christ").
2: A revelation of God: "Clouds and thick darkness are around him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne." Hebrews 1:8 — "Your throne, O God, is forever and ever, the scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your kingdom." The throne whose foundation is righteousness and justice is the throne on which Christ sits — the throne he shares with the Father (Revelation 3:21 — "the one who conquers, I will grant him to sit with me on my throne").
3: A revelation of God: "Fire goes before him and burns up his adversaries all around." 2 Thessalonians 1:7-8 — Christ will be "revealed from heaven with his mighty angels in flaming fire, inflicting vengeance on those who do not know God." Hebrews 12:29 — "our God is a consuming fire." The fire that goes before the LORD is the fire of the holiness of Christ at his coming.
4: A revelation of God: "His lightnings light up the world; the earth sees and trembles." Matthew 24:27 — "as the lightning comes from the east and shines as far as the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man." Revelation 16:18 — "flashes of lightning, rumblings, peals of thunder, and a great earthquake." The theophanic imagery of Ps 97 is the imagery of Christ's second coming.
5: A revelation of God: "The mountains melt like wax before the LORD, before the Lord of all the earth." Revelation 16:20 — "every island fled away, and no mountains were to be found." 2 Peter 3:12 — "the day of God... because of which the heavens will be set on fire and dissolved, and the heavenly bodies will melt as they burn!" The dissolution of creation before the LORD is the dissolution before the returning Christ.
6: A revelation of God: "The heavens proclaim his righteousness, and all the peoples see his glory." Revelation 1:7 — "Behold, he is coming with the clouds, and every eye will see him, even those who pierced him, and all tribes of the earth will wail on account of him." 2 Corinthians 4:6 — "the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ." Every eye will see the glory at Christ's return.
7: A direct: "worship him, all you gods!" Hebrews 1:6 — "when he brings the firstborn into the world, he says, 'Let all God's angels worship him.'" This is a direct NT application of the command to heavenly beings to worship Christ at his exaltation/incarnation. Colossians 1:15-16 — Christ is above all principalities and powers. The "gods" commanded to worship in Psalms 97:7 are the angels commanded to worship the Son in Hebrews 1:6.
8: A shadow: "Zion hears and is glad, and the daughters of Judah rejoice, because of your judgments, O LORD." Luke 1:46-55 — Mary's Magnificat rejoices at God's judgment on the proud and his mercy on the lowly. Luke 2:14 — "glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased!" The daughters of Judah rejoicing at the LORD's coming find their fullest expression in those who welcome the incarnate Son.
9: A direct: "For you, O LORD, are most high over all the earth; you are exalted far above all gods." Philippians 2:9 — "God has highly exalted him and bestowed on him the name that is above every name." Ephesians 1:21 — Christ is seated at the right hand of God "far above all rule and authority and power and dominion, and above every name that is named." The "most high over all the earth" and "exalted far above all gods" is the exalted Christ.
10: A shadow: "He preserves the lives of his saints; he delivers them from the hand of the wicked." John 10:28-29 — "I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish; no one will snatch them out of my hand." 1 Peter 1:5 — "who by God's power are being guarded through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed." Christ is the keeper who preserves his own through every danger, even death itself.
11: A shadow: "Light is sown for the righteous, and joy for the upright in heart." John 8:12 — "I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life." John 15:11 — "these things I have spoken to you, that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be full." The light and joy that are "sown" for the righteous are sown in Christ — they spring up in the resurrection and flower fully in the new creation.
12: A shadow: "Rejoice in the LORD, O you righteous, and give thanks to his holy name!" Philippians 4:4 — "Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, rejoice." Colossians 3:17 — "whatever you do, in word or deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him." The "holy name" in which the righteous rejoice and give thanks is the name of Jesus, the Lord.
